newsSince: Genuinely new evidence post-June 9: around June 18-19 Wolfspeed rolled out 5th-gen 200mm SiC MOSFETs and new 3.3kV SiC power-module families explicitly targeting AI data centers, grid-scale and solid-state transformers, and the GE Aerospace high-voltage SiC MOU was reframed; stock spiked ~+15% one day. Balance sheet still distressed (net loss -$519.6M, negative FCF) post Sept-2025 Ch.11 emergence; +168% YTD on a $2.7B cap.

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent power/component node: new 3.3kV AI-data-center SiC modules + Gen-5 200mm MOSFETs strengthen the strategic optionality this page tracks since June 9, but distress (negative FCF, -$520M loss) still overwhelms it. Modest fundamental tick; stays deep in tail.
verdicts.bull: Real new AI-datacenter SiC product cadence + GE MOU validate the power-conversion node.
verdicts.neutral: Optionality is real but balance-sheet fragility caps any rerating.
verdicts.bear: Post-bankruptcy equity, -168% YTD froth, Chinese SiC share gains erode moat.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: 3.3kV/Gen-5 SiC design wins; SiC demand recovery

verdicts.rankRationale: CPO/HBM/advanced-packaging/test lens: the new SiC modules and GE MOU are high-voltage power, orthogonal to co-packaged optics. No page-relevant evidence since June 9; +168% YTD on a $2.7B distressed cap keeps asymmetry poor here, so removal bias stands.
verdicts.bull: AI-power optionality could tangentially benefit datacenter buildout.
verdicts.neutral: Power SiC, not optics/packaging - wrong lens for this page.
verdicts.bear: No CPO relevance, distressed balance sheet, frothy YTD.

verdicts.rankRationale: Test-time/scaling power-density lens: new 3.3kV SiC modules for AI data centers and solid-state transformers plus Gen-5 200mm MOSFETs are directly on-thesis (faster time-to-power) - genuinely new since June 9, modestly strengthening the AI-power-density scaling case despite the speculative balance sheet.
verdicts.bull: SiC for solid-state transformers/AI power exactly matches the scaling power-density story.
verdicts.neutral: On-thesis product news, but tiny cap and distress limit conviction.
verdicts.bear: Negative FCF; demand recovery still unproven; Chinese SiC pressure.
